title: Part 2 – Long Turn (C1 Cambridge)
description: Evaluate whether you have speculated about the photographs or just described them, and analyze your C1 vocabulary.
prompt: Acts as an official examiner for the Cambridge C1 Advanced (CAE). Evaluate my response to Part 2 (Long Turn / photos) according to the official Cambridge criteria: Grammar and Vocabulary, Discourse Management, Pronunciation and Interactive Communication. Rate each criterion from 1 to 5. Check if I have speculated about feelings and situations (correct) or just described the photos literally (incorrect). Identify the fillers I have used and suggest alternatives C1. Next, give me 5 C1 level expressions that fit what I said. Here is my answer: [PASTE YOUR TEXT]

criteria.name: Grammar and Vocabulary
criteria.description: Use of speculation structures (must be, could be, might suggest) and sophisticated descriptive vocabulary.

criteria.name: Discourse Management
criteria.description: Clear organization of the comparison: identify similarities, differences and answer the proposed question.

criteria.name: Pronunciation
criteria.description: Fluidez al hablar durante el turno largo de aproximadamente 1 minuto sin pausas excesivas.

criteria.name: Interactive Communication
criteria.description: Capacidad de responder brevemente a la pregunta adicional dirigida al otro candidato.

tips: ESPECULA, no describas: en lugar de 'There is a person working', di 'The person seems to be deeply focused, which might suggest they're under considerable pressure.'
Use modals of speculation: 'must be', 'could be experiencing', 'might suggest', 'appears to be', 'seems as though'
Compara usando estructuras sofisticadas: 'While both images depict...', 'In contrast to the first photograph, the second...'
Answer the proposed question directly before ending your turn.
Use expressions to save time elegantly: 'What strikes me most about this image is...', 'Looking more closely at...'

speakingFeatures.duration: 1 minute per candidate (+ brief response from other candidate)
speakingFeatures.focus: Comparing photographs and speculating about them
speakingFeatures.examinerRole: Gives instructions and asks a follow-up question to the other candidate
speakingFeatures.commonMistake: Describing what you see literally instead of speculating about feelings, reasons, and implications
